coredata.dc:title: Electrochemical aptasensor based on gold modified thiol graphene as sensing platform and gold-palladium modified zirconium metal-organic frameworks nanozyme as signal enhancer for ultrasensitive detection of mercury ions
coredata.prism:publicationName: Journal of Colloid and Interface Science
coredata.dc:creator: Wang, Yaoguang
Wang, Yingying
Wang, Fangzheng
Chi, Hong
Zhao, Guanhui
Zhang, Yong
Li, Tianduo
Wei, Qin
coredata.dc:description: Gold modified thiol graphene (Au@HS-rGO) was prepared and applied as sensing platform for constructing the electrochemical aptasensor. While gold-palladium modified zirconium metal-organic frameworks (AuPd@UiO-67) nanozyme was employed as signal enhancer for detecting mercury ions (Hg2+) sensitively. Herein, gold nanoparticles (Au NPs) were modified on HS-rGO to form the thin Au@HS-rGO layer. Then the substrate strand (Apt1) was modified on the platform through Au-S bond. The signal strand (Apt2) was further decorated on the platform in the presence of Hg2+. Herein, the Apt2 was labeled with AuPd@UiO-67 nanozyme, which exhibited catalase-like properties to catalyze H2O2, thereby generating the electrical signal. With the concentration of Hg2+ increased, the amount of modified Apt2-AuPd@UiO-67 increased, leading to the rise of current response. Since the current responses were linear with concentration of Hg2+, the detection of Hg2+ can be achieved. Under the optimum conditions, the prepared electrochemical aptasensor exhibited wide linear range from 1.0 nmol/L to 1.0 mmol/L, along with a low detection limit of 0.16 nmol/L. Moreover, the electrochemical aptasensor showed excellent selectivity, reproducibility and stability, together with superior performance in actual water sample analysis. Therefore, this proposed electrochemical aptasensor may have promising applications and provide references for environmental monitoring and management.
coredata.dcterms:subject: AuPd@UiO-67 nanozyme
Au@HS-rGO
Electrochemical aptasensor
Detection
Mercury ions

Characterization of the electrode modification process
Other features of the prepared aptasensor

ZR2020QB094 , 2019GSF111023 ), National Key Scientific Instrument and Equipment Development Project of China (No. 21627809), and Y.G.W. thanks the Program for Scientific Research Innovation Team in Colleges and Universities of Shandong Province.

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: (A) Diagrammatic sketch for preparing Apt2-AuPd@UiO-67; (B) Schematic diagram for fabricating the designed electrochemical aptasensor.
SEM images of Au@HS-rGO (A), UiO-67 (B) and AuPd@UiO-67 nanozyme (C); EDS analysis of AuPd@UiO-67 nanozyme (D).

Electrochemical impedance spectra (EIS) of the modification processes of the electrode at 2.5 mmol/L [Fe(CN)6]3-/4- accompanied with 0.1 mol/L KCl: GCE (a), GCE/Au@HS-rGO (b), GCE/Au@HS-rGO/Apt1 (c), GCE/Au@HS-rGO/Apt1/MCH (d), GCE/Au@HS-rGO/Apt1/MCH/Hg2+ (e), GCE/Au@HS-rGO/Apt1/MCH/Hg2+/Apt2-AuPd@UiO-67 (f).
The effect of pH (A), amount of Au@HS-rGO (B) and incubation temperature (C) for the performance of the fabricated electrochemical aptasensor. Error bar = SD (n = 5).

Current responses (A) and working curve (B) of the electrochemical aptasensor for detecting different concentrations of Hg2+, from a to g: 1.0 nmol/L, 10 nmol/L, 100 nmol/L, 1.0 μmol/L, 10 μmol/L, 100 μmol/L, 1.0 mmol/L; Current change (C) of the fabricated aptasensor for detecting 100 nmol/L of Hg2+, blank sample and 10 μmol/L of Al3+, Ba2+, Cu2+, Fe2+, Mg2+, respectively; (D) Reproducibility of the prepared aptasensor in detecting 100 nmol/L of Hg2+. Error bar = SD (n = 5).
Performance of the fabricated aptasensor compared with reported methods for the detection of Hg2+.

Electrochemical aptasensor based on Au@HS-rGO and AuPd@UiO-67 nanozyme

head.ce:title: Electrochemical aptasensor based on gold modified thiol graphene as sensing platform and gold-palladium modified zirconium metal-organic frameworks nanozyme as signal enhancer for ultrasensitive detection of mercury ions

ce:affiliation.ce:label: a

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Collaborative Innovation Center for Green Chemical Manufacturing and Accurate Detection, Key Laboratory of Interfacial Reaction & Sensing Analysis in Universities of Shandong, School of Chemistry and Chemical Engineering, University of Jinan, Jinan 250022, People’s Republic of China

Key Laboratory of Interfacial Reaction & Sensing Analysis in Universities of Shandong
School of Chemistry and Chemical Engineering

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Collaborative Innovation Center for Green Chemical Manufacturing and Accurate Detection, Key Laboratory of Interfacial Reaction & Sensing Analysis in Universities of Shandong, School of Chemistry and Chemical Engineering, University of Jinan, Jinan 250022, People’s Republic of China

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: Gold modified thiol graphene (Au@HS-rGO) was prepared and applied as sensing platform for constructing the electrochemical aptasensor. While gold-palladium modified zirconium metal-organic frameworks (AuPd@UiO-67) nanozyme was employed as signal enhancer for detecting mercury ions (Hg2+) sensitively. Herein, gold nanoparticles (Au NPs) were modified on HS-rGO to form the thin Au@HS-rGO layer. Then the substrate strand (Apt1) was modified on the platform through Au-S bond. The signal strand (Apt2) was further decorated on the platform in the presence of Hg2+. Herein, the Apt2 was labeled with AuPd@UiO-67 nanozyme, which exhibited catalase-like properties to catalyze H2O2, thereby generating the electrical signal. With the concentration of Hg2+ increased, the amount of modified Apt2-AuPd@UiO-67 increased, leading to the rise of current response. Since the current responses were linear with concentration of Hg2+, the detection of Hg2+ can be achieved. Under the optimum conditions, the prepared electrochemical aptasensor exhibited wide linear range from 1.0 nmol/L to 1.0 mmol/L, along with a low detection limit of 0.16 nmol/L. Moreover, the electrochemical aptasensor showed excellent selectivity, reproducibility and stability, together with superior performance in actual water sample analysis. Therefore, this proposed electrochemical aptasensor may have promising applications and provide references for environmental monitoring and management.

ce:section.ce:para: In recent decades, with the rapid development of industry, mercury content in the environment is increasing. Mercury content in various environmental media and food especially fish on the earth has exhibited a serious impact on the environment [1,2]. As one of the heavy metal pollutants, mercury ions (Hg2+) also possesses a great threat to human health when entering the human body through drinking water [3,4]. It can cause many diseases including damage to the brain and central nervous system, kidney failure, cardiovascular, immune system damage, et al. Even trace amounts of Hg2+ can have serious effects on the human body [5–7]. The United States Environmental Protection Agency (U. S. EPA) has declared that the permissible content of Hg2+ in drinking water is 10 nmol/L maximally [8]. Therefore, the sensitive detection of Hg2+ is of great importance for protecting human health.

In recent years, a number of methods have been applied to detect Hg2+ including atomic absorption spectroscopy (AAS) [9], inductively coupled plasma atomic emission spectrometry (ICP-AES) [10], inductively coupled plasma mass spectrometry (ICP-MS) [11], UV–visible spectrophotometry (UV–Vis) [12] and atomic fluorescence spectrometry (AFS) [13]. Nevertheless, most of the above methods need expensive instruments and complicated operational processes. Hence, it is necessary to develop a sensitive, simple and low-cost method to detect Hg2+.

Electrochemical aptasensor has been proven to be a sensitive method for the detection of biomolecules and small molecules, which has gained extensive attention from researchers [14–17]. As is well-known, the electrode modified material and the applied labels play a vital role in affecting the quality of the prepared electrochemical aptasensor [18,19]. Reduced graphene oxide (rGO) has been recognized as an outstanding electrode modified material thanks to its excellent electrical conductivity and large specific surface area [20–24]. In order to better play the role of platform, the functionalization of rGO such as amination (–NH2), carboxylation (–COOH) or sulfhydrylation (-SH) is necessary. In addition, gold nanoparticles (Au NPs) could connect with the functional groups of the functionalized rGO, further improving the performance of the sensing platform. Moreover, the applied labels could undoubtedly influence the property of electrochemical aptasensor [25,26]. Nanozyme is one kind of mimic enzyme with the unique properties of nanomaterials and catalytic function, which owns the characteristics of high catalytic efficiency, stable, economic and large-scale preparation, and has been widely used in the fields of medicine [27], chemical industry [28], food [29], agriculture [30] and environment [31]. Specifically, noble metal nanoparticles including gold nanoparticles (Au NPs) and palladium nanoparticles (Pd NPs) as nanozyme have exhibited the properties of peroxidase, catalase, oxidase and superoxide dismutase [32–35]. Furthermore, metal organic frameworks (MOFs) as an emerging material have gained great attention thanks to its controllable pore size, functional groups and good biocompatibility [36–39]. It is also widely used as supporting matrix for immobilization of the nanoparticles [40]. In this case, large amounts of nanoparticles can be modified on the surface of the MOFs, better playing the function of nanoparticles.

In this work, an ultrasensitive electrochemical aptasensor which applied gold modified thiol graphene (Au@HS-rGO) as sensing platform and gold-palladium modified zirconium metal-organic frameworks (AuPd@UiO-67) nanozyme as signal enhancer for sensitive detection of Hg2+ was manufactured. Herein, Au NPs were loaded on HS-rGO to modify the glassy carbon electrode (GCE), acting as the sensing platform. The Au NPs performed connection function between HS-rGO and the following modified substrate strand (Apt1). Then the 6-mercapto-1-hexanol (MCH) was decorated on the electrode to seal the nonspecific active sites. Afterwards, various concentrations of Hg2+ were embellished on the electrode. Eventually, the AuPd@UiO-67 nanozyme labeled with signal strand (Apt2) was modified on the electrode through the T-Hg(II)-T structure. The current signal would be acquired by means of the catalytic action of AuPd@UiO-67 nanozyme toward H2O2. With the concentration of modified Hg2+ raised, the amount of Apt2-AuPd@UiO-67 nanozyme modified on electrode increased, thereby realizing the detection of Hg2+. Here the application of AuPd@UiO-67 nanozyme as signal enhancer to label DNA strand for achieving the sensitive detection of Hg2+ was reported firstly. Furthermore, the prepared electrochemical aptasensor displayed outstanding selectivity, reproducibility as well as stability in detecting Hg2+. Besides, the obtained electrochemical aptasensor exhibited superior performance in actual water sample analysis, providing basis for environmental monitoring and protection.

Firstly, 2 mg of HS-rGO was dispersed into 1 mL of ultrapure water and dealt with ultrasonic for 3.5 h. Then the obtained solution was mixed with 1.45 mL of 0.8 mol/L ascorbic acid, 0.5 mL of 2.0 mol/L polyvinylpyrrolidone (PVP) and 0.5 mL of 2% HAuCl4, stirring for 3 h at 90 °C. Finally, the mixture was washed with ultrapure water at 8000 rpm for 8 min. And the obtained Au@HS-rGO was dispersed into 1 mL of ultrapure water and stored at 4 °C ready for use.

Two kinds of precursor solution were prepared. Firstly, 45 mg of 2,2′-bipyridine-5,5′-dicarboxylic acid (BPDC) was added into 20 mL of N, N-dimethylformamide (DMF). Then 360 μL of triethylamine was added into the above mixture and the first precursor solution would be obtained. Secondly, 45 mg of ZrCl4 was dissolved into 16 mL of DMF, which was the secondary precursor solution. Afterwards, the above two precursor solutions were mixed, following the addition of 2.25 mL of acetic acid. Then the mixture was transferred into sealed autoclave and maintained at 85 °C for 24 h. After the reaction stopped, the mixture was centrifuged at 7800 rpm for 15 min. Furthermore, the resulting solid was rinsed with DMF, methanol and ethanol. Finally, the solid was vacuum dried at 35 °C to acquire the product.

Firstly, 0.25 mL of HAuCl4 (2%) and 0.5 mL of Na2PdCl4 (1%) were added into 1 mL of UiO-67 solution (1 mg/mL) with the vigorous ultrasonic for 20 min. Then 2 mL of 0.1 mol/L of NaBH4 was mixed with the above-mentioned solution dropwise. After stirring of 30 min, the obtained product was rinsed with ultrapure water three times by centrifugation and dispersed in ultrapure water (1 mL) for subsequent use.

The schematic diagram for preparing the Apt2-AuPd@UiO-67 is shown in Fig. 1 (A). Firstly, 20 μL of Tris(2-carboxyethyl)phosphine (TCEP, 1 mmol/L) was added into Apt2 solution (200 μL) and the mixture was stored in the dark for 1 h at room temperature to destroy the disulfide bond. Afterwards, the mixture was blent with 1 mL of AuPd@UiO-67 solution (2 mg/mL) and kept stirring gently at 4 °C for 12 h. Subsequently, the mixture was centrifuged at 8000 rpm and 4 °C for 5 min and washed with ultrapure water. Eventually, the obtained Apt2-AuPd@UiO-67 was dispersed in hybridization solution with pH 7.4 (1 mL) and stored at 4 °C for further use.

At first, 15 μL of TCEP (10 mmol/L) was mixed with 300 μL of Apt1 solution (10 μmol/L) and stored at ambient temperature in the dark for 1 h to destroy the disulfide bond. The GCE was disposed with 1.0 μm, 0.5 μm and 0.05 μm alumina powder, followed washing with ultrapure water. Observed from Fig. 1(B), 8 μL of Au@HS-rGO (2 mg/mL) was modified on the surface of GCE as substrate. After drying at ambient temperature and washing with ultrapure water, the Apt1 solution (6 μL, 10 μmol/L) was mixed with the previously prepared electrode and hatched at 4 °C for 1.5 h. Then 5 μL of MCH was modified on the electrode and stored at 4 °C for 1.5 h to seal the non-specific binding sites. After the prepared electrode was washed, it was modified with a certain concentration of Hg2+ and reacted at 4 °C for 1 h. Thereafter, the above prepared Apt2-AuPd@UiO-67 mixture (6 μL) was overlie onto the surface of electrode and hatched for 2 h at 4 °C. Eventually, the obtained completely modified electrode was rinsed by using ultrapure water and stored at 4 °C for subsequently testing.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Characterization of Au@HS-rGO, UiO-67 and AuPd@UiO-67 nanozyme
ce:section.ce:para: Fig. 2A showed the scanning electron microscope (SEM) image for Au@HS-rGO. Observed from the image, the HS-rGO exhibited thin layer structure with translucent state. Meanwhile, large amounts of Au nanoparticles were loaded on it, indicating the successful synthesis of Au@HS-rGO. The SEM image of UiO-67 was shown in Fig. 2B. It is obvious that the UiO-67 displayed irregular spherical structure with the average diameter of 110 nm. As can be seen from Fig. 2C, a great number of Au and Pd nanoparticles were loaded onto UiO-67, manifesting the successful synthesis of AuPd@UiO-67 nanozyme. In addition, energy dispersive spectroscopy (EDS) analysis of AuPd@UiO-67 nanozyme (Fig. 2 D) exhibited that the elements of Au, Pd, C, O, Cl and Zr were contained in it, further indicating the successful preparation of AuPd@UiO-67 nanozyme.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Characterization of the electrode modification process
ce:section.ce:para: Electrochemical impedance spectra (EIS) has been proved to be one of the most forceful tools for exploring the surface properties of the prepared electrodes [41–43]. The impedance spectra contain semicircle part and linear part. The semicircle part at high frequencies represents the limited process of electron transfer, and the linear part at low frequencies corresponds to the limited process of diffusion. Meanwhile, the diameter of the semicircle corresponds to the resistance of electron transfer. In order to better demonstrate the construction processes of the aptasensor, EIS was applied to investigate the processes of surface layer modification (Fig. 3 ). As shown in Fig. 3(a), the bare GCE showed a near linear spectrum, indicating the good conductivity of it. When Au@HS-rGO was embellished on electrode (curve b), the semicircle diameter increased, which indicated the successful modification of Au@HS-rGO on GCE. After the modification of Apt1 (curve c), the resistance continued increasing, which was due that Apt1 as non-electroactive substance hindered the electron transfer. Then the combination of MCH increased the electrode resistance further (curve d). Afterwards, the semicircle diameter increased obviously after the embellishment of Hg2+ (curve e), manifesting the triumphant immobilization of Hg2+ on GCE. Eventually, after the immobilization of Apt2-AuPd@UiO-67 (curve f), the resistance further increased. All the above results revealed that the fabrication of the aptasensor was successful.

To enable the prepared electrochemical aptasensor to detect Hg2+ under optimal conditions and obtain the maximum current response, the experimental conditions were optimized. As can be seen in Fig. 4 A, the current responses raised with the increasing pH value at low pH. While at high pH, the current responses decreased with the increasing pH value. When the pH value was at about 7.4, the current signal reached the maximum value. Hence the pH 7.4 PBS solution was chosen for the succeeding study. Additionally, the quantity of Au@HS-rGO modified on the electrode also affected the current signal of the aptasensor. Fig. 4B displayed the influence of different amounts of Au@HS-rGO toward the performance of the prepared aptasensor. While the quantity of Au@HS-rGO was from 5 μL to 8 μL, the current responses obviously increased with the increase of Au@HS-rGO volume. And the current responses reduced while the volume of Au@HS-rGO was more than 8 μL, which may be due that excessive substrate would cause excessive resistance on the electrode surface, leading to the diminution of the electrical signal. Since the current responses reached maximum when the substrate volume was 8 μL, it was chosen as the optimum volume in the subsequent test. Moreover, the incubation temperature for the fabricated aptasensor was also optimized. Observed from Fig. 4C, the current responses rose with the incubation temperature in the range of 5 – 35 °C, which indicated that the increasing temperature promoted the binding of biomolecules. When the temperature was above 35 °C, the current responses decreased gradually, which may be due that the relatively high temperatures would destroy the structures of biomolecules. Therefore, the incubation temperature of 35 °C was selected for the subsequent studies.

With the optimum conditions of pH 7.4 PBS and the substrate Au@HS-rGO dosage of 8 μL, the fabricated electrochemical aptasensor was constructed to detect different concentrations of Hg2+. Herein, the amperometric i-t curves were recorded after the injection of H2O2 (Fig. 5 A). It is obvious that the proposed aptasensor exhibited a linear operating range of 1.0 nmol/L − 1.0 mmol/L (Fig. 5B). The equation of the calibration curve was I = 30.68 lgc + 24.96, with the linear coefficient of 0.9970. And the detection limit was 0.16 nmol/L, which was calculated on the basis of international union of pure and applied chemistry (IUPAC) [44]. And the detailed computational process was provided in supporting information . The property of the fabricated aptasensor was compared with some reported methods previously. Observed from Table 1 , the fabricated aptasensor showed a better property than some other reports. The reasons for the prepared aptasensor owning such good property could be summarized as folllows: First of all, the Au@HS-rGO with thin layer structure could provide a large number of Au nanoparticles, which was beneficial for the immobilization of Apt1. Second, the AuPd@UiO-67 nanozyme as signal enhancer performed excellent catalase-like catalytic property toward H2O2, thus improving the sensibility of the aptasensor. Third, the sensing strategy that designed could specifically identify Hg2+, realizing the selective and sensitive detection of it.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Other features of the prepared aptasensor
ce:section.ce:para: The selectivity testing plays an important part in assessing the constructed biosensors. In order to guarantee that the fabricated aptasensor could test precisely, the interference tests were carried out. Herein, the 100 nmol/L of Hg2+ and 10 μmol/L of potential interfering ions including Al3+, Ba2+, Cu2+, Fe2+, Mg2+ were applied to construct the aptasensor. As Fig. 5C shown, the detection of 100 nmol/L of Hg2+ produced a large current signal, while the addition of the interfering ions generated almost no current response. The obtained results manifested that the fabricated aptasensor exhibited an excellent selectivity.

The reproducibility of biosensors is an important index to evaluate whether they have practical application value. In this work, we prepared five electrochemical aptasensors with the same experimental situation and their electrical responses were tested (Fig. 5D). The relative standard deviation (RSD) of the testing results with five times was below 5%, demonstrating that the prepared electrochemical aptasensor owned good reproducibility.

In addition, the stability of the aptasensor was also tested. An electrochemical aptasensor was fabricated and the current signal was recorded. While not in use, the obtained aptasensor was kept at 4 °C. After 1, 3, 5, 7 days of testing, the electrochemical response signal decreased within acceptable range, indicating that the stability of the aptasensor was acceptable.

For verifying the practical application value of the designed electrochemical aptasensor, standard addition method was employed for detecting Hg2+ in tap water and lake water. Before the test, the water samples were treated through centrifugation and filtration. As shown in Table S1, the recovery of the test was in the range of 99.36% − 101.7% for tap water and 99.96% − 103.5% for lake water. And the RSD ranged from 2.35% to 4.56% and from 3.33% to 4.29%, respectively. According to the above results, we can conclude that the aptasensor which we constructed had good performance in actual water sample detection.

An ultrasensitive electrochemical aptasensor was prepared by using Au@HS-rGO as sensing platform and AuPd@UiO-67 nanozyme as signal enhancer for sensitive detection of Hg2+ for the first time. The AuPd@UiO-67 nanozyme showed excellent catalase-like performance toward H2O2, providing possibilities for the sensitive detection of Hg2+. Meanwhile, the T-Hg(II)-T structure guaranteed the feasibility of the proposed strategy, achieving the well modification of Hg2+. The proposed electrochemical aptasensor showed a wide linear range (1.0 nmol/L − 1.0 mmol/L) and low detection limit (0.16 nmol/L) for detecting Hg2+, exhibiting better performance compared with some other reported methods [45–51]. Furthermore, the fabricated electrochemical aptasensor displayed excellent selectivity, reproducibility and stability, along with a superior performance in actual water sample analysis. Nevertheless, the designed strategy could only detect single metal ion. We will explore the simultaneous detection of multiple metal ions on the same interface in the future. In general, this study may offer a reference for the detection of other heavy metal ions and may find promising application in the field of environmental monitoring.

Yaoguang Wang: Conceptualization, Methodology, Investigation, Supervision, Writing – original draft, Writing – review & editing, Project administration. Yingying Wang: Investigation, Data curation, Formal analysis. Fangzheng Wang: Investigation, Data curation. Hong Chi: Visualization. Guanhui Zhao: Conceptualization, Methodology, Data curation, Formal analysis, Supervision. Yong Zhang: Visualization, Formal analysis. Tianduo Li: Conceptualization, Supervision. Qin Wei: Conceptualization, Supervision.

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: This study was supported by the National Natural Science Foundation of China (Nos. 22006080, 21777056, 21775053), Shandong Provincial Natural Science Foundation (Nos. ZR2020QB094, 2019GSF111023), National Key Scientific Instrument and Equipment Development Project of China (No. 21627809), and Y.G.W. thanks the Program for Scientific Research Innovation Team in Colleges and Universities of Shandong Province.

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para: Supplementary data to this article can be found online at https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jcis.2021.08.055.

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para:
The following are the Supplementary data to this article:
Supplementary Data 1

J. Colloid Interf. Sci.
